title: Habenaria constricta
taxonomy: Habenaria constricta is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Cranichideae. The genus Habenaria is a large and diverse group of orchids, with over 700 species worldwide. The classification of Habenaria constricta reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the orchid family. Further research may be necessary to fully understand its classification and relationships to other species.
uses: Habenaria constricta is a species of orchid with reported medicinal uses. Its roots are traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues in various parts of the world. The plant is also used to relieve coughs and other respiratory problems. In some cultures, it is used as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses and their safety for human consumption.

parts: Habenaria constricta, commonly known as the Constricted Habenaria or Constricted Orchid, is a species of orchid found in tropical regions. intro: Habenaria constricta, commonly known as the Constricted Habenaria, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria constricta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Habenaria constricta include decoctions and infusions, often used to make tea or tonics that are consumed to alleviate respiratory problems and boost the immune system.
